grading_rubric: Emotion (visceral stop-scroll) + Value (concrete promise) + Credibility (specific named proof), each 1-10. Total out of 30. Ship >= 23, Rewrite 19-22, Kill < 19.

ideas.idea_summary: Rolex post autopsy — break down what separated the 8 builders from the 392 talkers.

hooks.text: LinkedIn told me this post reached 400,000 people. 312 of them sent a message. 8 of them sent something they built. That's the actual hiring funnel.

emotion.reasoning: Number-ladder creates curiosity; 'actual hiring funnel' dangles the framework.

value.reasoning: Promises a full funnel breakdown — actionable for anyone hiring.

credibility.reasoning: First-person, specific three-stage number, no fluff.

hooks.text: I'm about to hire someone who sent me a Loom at 3 AM. The 312 people who sent resumes are still waiting.

emotion.reasoning: Visceral contrast; 3 AM + resumes-still-waiting is a vibe.

value.reasoning: Implies a lesson but doesn't promise a framework.

credibility.reasoning: Specific numbers and channel, but no named proof.

hooks.rewrite_suggestion: Tighten second beat to name the takeaway: '...are still waiting. The filter is the product you ship before the interview.'

hooks.text: You've probably never heard of the person I'm hiring. They shipped working software 14 hours after my post went live.

value.reasoning: Implied lesson (build fast) but not concretely promised.

credibility.reasoning: 14-hour window is specific; rest is thin.

hooks.rewrite_suggestion: Add a second line naming the tool or the insight so value stops being abstract.

hooks.text: One LinkedIn post. 400 applicants. 8 of them did the one thing that mattered. Here's the filter.

credibility.reasoning: Numbers good; 'the one thing' flirts with cliché.

hooks.text: When I posted the Rolex hire, I had no application system, no filter, no plan for 400 DMs. Then one person sent a Loom. The filter built itself.

value.reasoning: Origin of a filter; value is implied.

ideas.idea_summary: Anti-vanity case study — a $50M founder with 3K followers out-pipelined a 200K-follower founder. Explain why.

hooks.text: Sequoia flagged a founder to me last month with 3,247 LinkedIn followers. Same week I took a meeting with a 210K-follower founder. Guess which one closed $2M in pipeline.
I charge founders $15K a month to give them fewer followers. The ones who understand why generate 10x the pipeline of their competitors.

emotion.reasoning: Outrageous premise inverts every LinkedIn assumption.

value.reasoning: Invites the framework behind the claim.

credibility.reasoning: No named client; $15K pricing is new disclosure.

hooks.flag: Verify $15K pricing is okay to disclose publicly.

hooks.text: You've probably never heard of the $50M founder we write for. He has 3K LinkedIn followers. Last quarter he generated $2M in pipeline from one post.
$2M in pipeline from 3,000 followers. $0 in pipeline from 200,000. One line of copy explains the difference.

emotion.reasoning: Side-by-side contrast at its most brutal.

hooks.text: When this founder signed with us, he had 3K followers and two dying client accounts. 90 days later, he had 3,100 followers and $2M in new pipeline.

emotion.reasoning: Classic underdog arc; weaker kick than 2.4.

ideas.idea_summary: Hook autopsy — 2M-impression post generated one meeting, 4K-impression post generated $240K. Show the 6-word difference.

hooks.text: LinkedIn's algorithm handed one of my posts 2M impressions last month. One meeting. Another post got 4,000 impressions. $240K in pipeline. Here's the six-word difference.

value.reasoning: Six-word delta is the most concrete promise possible.

hooks.text: I tell founders to post things that 99% of their audience will scroll past. That's how they close $240K deals.
hooks.rewrite_suggestion: Anchor with a named client tier to lift credibility: 'One of our Sequoia-backed CEOs closed $240K from a post 99% of his industry scrolled past.'

hooks.text: You've probably never seen the post that generated $240K for my client. 4,000 people did. One of them signed a contract.
2,000,000 impressions. One meeting. Versus 4,000 impressions and $240K in pipeline. Same guy. Same month.

emotion.reasoning: Fragment punch; same-guy reveal lands hard.

value.reasoning: Promises why same person produced both.

hooks.text: When this CEO wrote his first post with us, he optimized for his whole industry. Zero replies. We cut 14 words. He wrote for 40 buyers. $240K closed in two weeks.

ideas.idea_summary: Bianca hire reframed — the 3 questions Shamus asked to hire his first writer in 90 seconds.

hooks.text: The New York Post called Dealroom the 'Media Mafia.' My first hire was made in 90 seconds on a phone call at 9 PM from Boston.

credibility.reasoning: NY Post, 90 seconds, 9 PM, Boston — four anchors.

hooks.text: I hired my first writer in 90 seconds. She flew to NYC in 48 hours. That's faster than most companies schedule a phone screen.
You've probably never heard of Bianca Drouin. She writes for founders backed by Sequoia and a16z. I hired her in 90 seconds.
90 seconds. 3 questions. 1 hire. That's how Bianca went from Boston student to writing for $1B+ founders.

value.reasoning: '3 questions' is the most concrete promise — readers will stay for them.

hooks.text: When Bianca DMd me, she had no portfolio, no degree in writing, no referral. She had one line: 'I'll outwork anyone.' I hired her that night.

ideas.idea_summary: Scaling mystique — how 5 writers serve founders backed by top VCs. Reveal the voice-engine system.

hooks.text: Forbes asked me how 5 people write LinkedIn posts for founders backed by a16z, Sequoia, Benioff, and Kevin Durant. The answer isn't a CMS. It's a voice engine.

emotion.reasoning: Anchor plus reveal, but Forbes-asked is unverified.

hooks.rewrite_suggestion: Drop the fabricated anchor. Replace with a real prompt: 'Three different founders asked me last week how 5 people write posts for companies backed by a16z...'

hooks.text: We run a $1.5M business with 5 writers. No AI replaces them. But one tool makes each of them carry 3 more clients without losing voice fidelity.
You've probably never seen the internal tool we built at Dealroom. It turns 20 of a founder's posts into a voice profile our writers ship against in minutes.
hooks.note: Doubles as a meta-hook for the Loom itself.

hooks.text: $1.5M ARR. 5 writers. 12 CEOs. The system fits on one screen.
When we hit 8 clients, voice drift killed a contract. We couldn't scale writers without losing the thing clients paid us for. So we built the voice engine. 12 clients later, zero churn.

emotion.reasoning: Pain-point lead; concrete failure anchors it.
